instruction: If you could help me write an email to my friends inviting them to dinner on Friday, it would be greatly appreciated.
output:
Subject: Dinner Invitation - A Special Evening with Friends!

Dear [friend's name],

I hope this email finds you well and in good health. I am writing to invite you for a special evening of delicious food, great company, and fun conversation at our place next Friday (date). It has been quite some time since we all got together as a group, and I believe that now is the perfect time to catch up over home-cooked meals and lively discussions.

The menu for the night includes [list of dishes] that have been carefully selected by myself based on your favorite cuisines/tastes from around the world. We will start off with appetizers like freshly baked bread paired with dips or sushi rolls before moving onto main courses such as grilled steak cooked medium rare accompanied by seasonal vegetables followed by decadent desserts made using locally sourced ingredients. There will also be plenty of wine available if anyone wants to indulge during mealtime conversations about life events past & present.

Please let me know ASAP whether you can make it so that I can plan accordingly; there are only 4 seats left after yours :) Looking forward to seeing everyone again soon – don't forget those fancy outfits because sometimes these occasions call for dressing nicely too!

Kind regards,

[your name]

instruction: What are the steps to using random (yet coherent) text as filler content within your Word document?
output:
Here's a list of possible options:
1. Use an online tool like [Lorem Ipsum](https://www.lipsum.com/) or one from this collection https://github.com/twinniesworld/filler-text . These tools generate pseudo words that can be pasted into any word processor, and they usually have some sort of formatting option so you don't need multiple paragraph tags for each generated line in order not to look too artificial; just make sure it fits with what else is on screen visually!
2. If there isn't enough space between two lines when inserting them manually - use tabulation key instead which will create more distance without having extra spaces at beginning / end every time after pressing enter button while typing new sentence ;-)  
3. You could also try generating sentences by hand if needed but keep their length similar throughout entire file otherwise reader might feel confused about where exactly he should focus attention because different parts may contain either long passages filled up randomly OR short ones created specifically based upon context around those particular phrases etcetera :)

Hi there! I'm [Your Name], and it is an absolute pleasure to connect with you on the professional platform of choice, LinkedIn. Period Dramas

The plots typically revolve around romantic relationships between characters from different classes within society during their respective eras; these love affairs may be complicated by class differences, political intrigue, war, rebellion, religious beliefs, cultural traditions, family dynamics, gender roles, power struggles, moral dilemmas, societal expectations – essentially any aspect which can provide rich material for character development while showcasing various aspects of life at this particular point in history.

instruction:
You should take the time to read the situation and problem carefully and give the best advice you can.

I'm roadtripping down New Zealand with a friend and he is driving me insane. He hasn't enjoyed all the tours we've been on and doesn't want to chat about ANYTHING. We're a week into our trip and this is the quietest I've ever been when hanging out with another person as he rebuffs all my attempts at conversation. He wears his airpods in the car and around the accommodation - not that I've kept trying to talk to him. Honestly, he is bringing some seriously bad vibes to everything and it's ruining my trip.
We don't have anything booked in yet for the South Island, so I'm planning to ditch him in Wellington at the ferry. Probably book my own car in Picton and just relax alone for the rest of the trip. (Just thinking about it is such sharp relief that it makes me feel emotional. I'll pay whatever price I have to just to get away from him.)
Obviously telling him he is ruining my trip with his negative energy isn't the best way to approach this.
Any advice for the best approach so we can split amicably? Or at least not have an argument that ends with very hurt feelings?
output:
Firstly, let your partner know how they are making you uncomfortable or unhappy during their behavior towards others while traveling together. It might be helpful if there was something specific mentioned like "When [partner] ignores everyone else except themselves by wearing headphones constantly" instead of simply saying 'they wear earbuds too much', because then both parties will understand what exactly has made one upset/annoyed without having any assumptions thrown onto either side.
If possible try talking through these issues before arriving back home; discussing things over video calls may help alleviate tension between each other better than face-to-face meetings due to lack of physical proximity which could lead to misunderstanding emotions more easily. If after speaking up nothing changes within reasonable terms consider looking further ahead regarding future travel plans where perhaps booking separate rooms would allow space needed individually rather than feeling forced along someone who does not share similar interests nor wants interaction outside personal devices.

oblique
output: The term "oblique" comes from Latin, where it means "slanting," "bent at an angle," or "deviating slightly off course." It is used in various fields such as geometry, photography, architecture, engineering, linguistics, etc. In mathematics (particularly Euclidean plane geometry), oblique refers to a line segment whose direction deviates by some fixed amount relative to another reference straight line called its axis; this relationship can be described using angles formed between lines intersected with one common point on their endpoints known as vertices .

Review 1: I'm not sure if it's just the fact that Ed Norton really grates on me in this film, but I never really bought into the idea of Fight Club being some kind of cultural phenomenon. Yes it's an innovative story, told via a strong script, engaging characters and intense cinematography. But is it really worthy of such high praise? Personally, I find it starts to fall apart halfway through and actually becomes quite tedious towards the end. Everything just becomes a little bit childish and contrived as the story loses touch with its base elements and the rage against the machine vibe takes over. I have huge respect for the way this film was made but, much like The Matrix, I feel like people attach way more importance to this movie than it deserves.
Review 2: The film tells the story of men who cannot adapt to the social order in today's world, under the leadership of Tyler Durden, to discover their natural instinct, masculinity, and to escape from their unhappy lives.
Review 3: Despite a good theme, great acting and important messages that this movie convey in an unorthodox way, I think it fails to connect the audience with the storyline and leaves him in a world of confusion. Although, majority of reviews find this movie entertaining and interesting, yet I would choose to be a minority that believes that this movie is extremely overrated.
Review 4: Is creating chaos, explosions and membership in a militant sect the way to get rid of material shackles ?! The visual effects are fascinating and the story is compelling until the second half. Unfortunately, it doesn't end well.

output: Breaking Bad is an American crime drama television series created and produced by Vince Gilligan, which ran from January 2008 to September 2013 on AMC. The show tells the story of Walter White (Bryan Cranston), a chemistry teacher who finds himself in financial trouble after being diagnosed with lung cancer. To secure his family's future financially following his death, he teams up with Jesse Pinkman (Aaron Paul), a former student turned drug dealer, to start producing and selling methamphetamine under the pseudonym "Heisenberg". Along the way, they face numerous challenges as their criminal enterprise grows more complex and dangerous.

Dead Poets Society is a 1989 American drama film directed by Peter Weir and written by Tom Schulman. The story revolves around an English literature teacher, John Keating (played by Robin Williams), who inspires his students at a conservative boys' boarding school to think independently and follow their passions. Throughout the film, we witness how Mr. Keating challenges traditional values and encourages creativity through poetry readings and discussions about life lessons.

For example, when Neil Perry commits suicide after being pressured by his father not to pursue acting career; there was use of extreme wide angle lens which emphasized isolation from everyone else present during crucial moment leading towards tragedy eventually happened later with him taking own life due to immense pressure he faced back home wherein family wanted only successful future without any failures whatsoever even if it meant sacrificing oneself passion & dreams altogether!

instruction:
Provide a Java solution to the following problem.

Given an integer n, return true if it is a power of three. Otherwise, return false.
An integer n is a power of three, if there exists an integer x such that n == 3^x.

Example 1:
In: n = 27
Out: true
Explanation: 27 = 3^3

Example 2:
In: n = 0
Out: false
Explanation: There is no x where 3^x = 0.

Example 3:
In: n = -1
Out: false
Explanation: There is no x where 3x = (-1).
 
Constraints:
-231 <= n <= 231 - 1
Follow up: Could you solve it without loops/recursion?
output:
def is_power_of_three(n):
    return n > 0 and (n & (n - 1)) == 0 and (n >> 1) % 3 == 0

Table: Person
| Column Name | Type     |
| personId          | int         |
| lastName         | varchar |

personId is the primary key column for this table.
This table contains information about the ID of some persons and their first and last names.
Table: Address
| Column Name | Type     |
| addressId        | int         |
| personId          | int         |
| city                   | varchar|
| state                | varchar |
addressId is the primary key column for this table.
Each row of this table contains information about the city and state of one person with ID = PersonId.
Write an SQL query to report the first name, last name, city, and state of each person in the Person table. If the address of a personId is not present in the Address table, report null instead.
Return the result table in any order.
Implementation:
select FirstName, LastName, City, State
from Person left join Address
on Person.PersonId = Address.PersonId

instruction:
Summarize the article you have been given in a brief manner.

Mathematics and art are related in a variety of ways. Mathematics has itself been described as an art motivated by beauty. Mathematics can be discerned in arts such as music, dance, painting, architecture, sculpture, and textiles. This article focuses, however, on mathematics in the visual arts.
Mathematics and art have a long historical relationship. Artists have used mathematics since the 4th century BC when the Greek sculptor Polykleitos wrote his Canon, prescribing proportions conjectured to have been based on the ratio 1:√2 for the ideal male nude. Persistent popular claims have been made for the use of the golden ratio in ancient art and architecture, without reliable evidence. In the Italian Renaissance, Luca Pacioli wrote the influential treatise De divina proportione (1509), illustrated with woodcuts by Leonardo da Vinci, on the use of the golden ratio in art. Another Italian painter, Piero della Francesca, developed Euclid's ideas on perspective in treatises such as De Prospectiva Pingendi, and in his paintings. The engraver Albrecht Dürer made many references to mathematics in his work Melencolia I. In modern times, the graphic artist M. C. Escher made intensive use of tessellation and hyperbolic geometry, with the help of the mathematician H. S. M. Coxeter, while the De Stijl movement led by Theo van Doesburg and Piet Mondrian explicitly embraced geometrical forms. Mathematics has inspired textile arts such as quilting, knitting, cross-stitch, crochet, embroidery, weaving, Turkish and other carpet-making, as well as kilim. In Islamic art, symmetries are evident in forms as varied as Persian girih and Moroccan zellige tilework, Mughal jali pierced stone screens, and widespread muqarnas vaulting.
output: Math plays crucial role throughout history within various aspects of artistic creation including design elements like composition principles or aesthetic choices that artists make during their creative process.

Such ideas have also sparked a debate on whether trade itself should be codified as a human right.
output: The Fair Trade (also referred to as 'Trade Justice') Movement aims to promote responsible practices within global commodity supply chains by ensuring that workers are treated fairly with respect to their wages, working conditions, health and safety while protecting the natural resources used during manufacturing processes through sustainable farming methods [1]. This approach has led to increased consumer awareness about ethical consumption choices which can help alleviate poverty among producers who often face unfair trading terms or exploitative labor arrangements when dealing with large corporations based primarily in developed countries like Europe & North America[2][3]. Furthermore, this growing consensus around fairness principles is now being considered alongside other fundamental rights such as accessibility towards education opportunities afforded under Article 26(b)of International Covenant On Economic Social And Cultural Rights - thereby suggesting an expansion beyond mere economic benefits into broader conceptions encompassing cultural autonomy too!

instruction: Give students tips on how to keep their nerves under control during class presentations.
output:
1. Preparation is key: Make sure you are well-prepared for your presentation, including practicing and memorizing the content of your speech. This will help reduce anxiety about forgetting important information or making mistakes while speaking.

2. Breathe deeply: Before entering the classroom, take a few deep breaths to calm your nerves. Practicing slow breathing techniques can also help relax your body and mind beforehand.

3. Visualize success: Imagine yourself giving an excellent presentation with confidence and poise. Focus on positive outcomes rather than negative thoughts that may arise due to nervousness.

4. Stay focused: During your presentation, try not to let distractions like unfamiliar faces or noise from other classes affect your performance. Remind yourself why you're there - it could be because someone believes in you!

5. Practice good posture: Stand tall but relaxed; avoid slouching as this might make you appear more anxious when standing up front. Maintaining eye contact with listeners helps establish rapport between speaker and audience members too.

6. Accept feedback graciously: If something goes wrong (and sometimes things do), don't beat yourself up over minor errors made by human beings who have feelings just like everyone else does :) Instead, accept constructive criticism gracefully so they know what areas need improvement without feeling attacked personally.

7. Don't compare yourself to others: It’s easy to get caught up comparing ourselves negatively against our peers – especially if we feel pressure coming from teachers/professors etcetera. Remember each individual has unique strengths & weaknesses which should never lead us down destructive paths such as self doubt or comparisonitis.

8. Know Your Audience: Familiarize yourself with those attending any given lecture session since knowing them better allows one time management skills necessary throughout entire process leading into actual event itself.

9. Be prepared mentally ahead Of Time: By visualising everything going right even though nothing ever truly works out exactly accordingly plan A,B,C scenarios whereby depending upon situation at hand appropriate response would ensue thus reducing chances associated risk factors related stress levels altogether.

10. Seek support From Others : Reach Out To Those Who Can Provide Encouragement Or Just Listen Care Fully Without Judgement As They Are There For You Too When Things Get Tough.

instruction: You can easily make your holidays more eco-friendly by following these simple steps:
output:
1. Plan ahead and choose sustainable travel options, such as staying at locally owned accommodations or choosing public transportation over private vehicles when possible.

2. Reduce waste during the holiday season - bring reusable shopping bags to avoid using plastic ones; use washable tablecloths instead of disposable paper napkins for meals with family members who are coming from far away places etc.

3. Consider donating unwanted items rather than throwing them out – you may be able to donate unused gifts that were given last year so they don't end up in a landfill this time around.

4. Use energy efficient lighting like LED bulbs throughout any decorating activities while also turning off lights whenever not needed (even if it is just briefly). This will help reduce electricity consumption significantly without sacrificing aesthetics too much.

5. Encourage others on social media platforms about how important environmental conservation efforts should always remain top priority even though we celebrate special occasions occasionally. It helps create awareness among people worldwide regarding importance behind adopting green lifestyle habits every day!

instruction: Give a grammar tip on how to end a German verb.
output:
To form the perfect tense in German, you need to add "-t" or "-te" after an infinitive ending in -e and before any personal pronouns (I/we), as well as adding either one of two suffixes depending on whether it's your first time using this particular action since waking up that day ("seit") + present participle (-nen) OR if not for some reason: past indicative participle (+zusammen mit dem Präsenspartizip):

1. If I have done something once today already seit ich jetzt schon etwas getan habe, then use Perfect Participle II with Seitzeitwort plus Infinitive without Noun Stem Ending Plus Personal Pronouns followed by t: Es ist mir gelungen das Haus zu bauen. Meine Freunde und ich sind unsere Arbeit beendet hatten / es war für mich geworden ein guter Tag.
2. Otherwise – if there has been no activity yet involving said verbsince I am awake now seit meiner Erscheinung her zuvor nicht dasselbe gemacht bin -- then simply put together Present Indicative Tense Verb Formation along with Preposition “mit” meaning“with”and Past Participle (“geglaubt”) which means“believed”: Das Geld kannst du heute ausheben; sie glaubten ihre Reise abgeschlossen hätten.

instruction: Suggest a recipe for a vegan dessert.
output:
Here's a delicious and simple chocolate mousse that is perfect for any occasion, including birthdays, parties, or just as an indulgent treat!

Ingredients:
- 1 cup unsweetened cocoa powder
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 2 Tablespoons cornstarch
- 6 large eggs (or plant-based equivalent)
- 5 ounces bittersweet chocolate, chopped into small pieces*
- Non-dairy whipped topping (*optional but recommended), divided in half**
- Plant-based milk of your choice***

Directions:
1. In a medium saucepan, combine the non-dairy milk with one third of the sugars over low heat until warmed throughout - do not boil it; set aside while you prepare the rest of the ingredients.

2. Using either electric beaters OR by hand using whisks on high speed*, beat together all remaining dry Ingredients except *whipping cream at room temperature till well combined & smooth looking mixture forms when scraping down sides every now and then after about five minutes beating time. If needed add more sweeteners accordingly so its taste suits yours likings. Set this mix ready beside stove top where hot liquid will soon join us.
  
3. Once heated up gently pour prepared milky solution from step #one onto our waiting eggless batter without interrupting their dance party going strong right there on mixing bowl surface. Whisk both these elements simultaneously ensuring they blend harmoniously like two lovers who have been long separated yet reunited again under same roof. This stage might take some patience since we want them blending seamlessly instead of lumps forming due to lack coordination between each other during process ;-) So keep working diligently towards achieving desired result :) You can also use immersion stick attachment if available which makes job easier especially near end stages before thickening occurs naturally once cooked enough amount has mixed inside already created base layer atop itself resulting soft fluffy texture similar what vanilla ice creams usually offer upon first bite.

output: Matilda is a children's novel written by Roald Dahl and published in 1988. It tells the story of a young girl named Matilda Wormwood, who lives with her parents, Harry and Zinnia, in a small town in England. Despite their poor parenting skills, which often lead to abuse or neglect, Matilda possesses an extraordinary intelligence combined with compassion for others. She learns how to read at age three through observing books on television while staying home from school due to illness; she becomes fascinated by literature as well as knowledge about science fiction authors like H.G. Wells and Jules Verne. As time goes on, it emerges that there may be more than meets the eye when dealing with Mr. and Mrs. Wormwood: they have been secretly dabbling in criminal activities involving counterfeit money printing!

instruction:
In the event that a person enjoyed the book that was given, please suggest other books that they might also like.

The Great Gatsby
output:
If someone enjoys The Great Gatsby by F. Scott Fitzgerald, here are some additional recommendations:

1. A Farewell to Arms by Ernest Hemingway - This classic novel about an American ambulance driver in Italy during World War I is another great example of his unique writing style and themes of love, loss, and fate.

2. To Kill a Mockingbird by Harper Lee - Set in 1930s Alabama, this Pulitzer Prize-winning novel tells the story of Scout Finch as she witnesses her father Atticus' defense of a black man accused of rape. It explores issues of race, class, and morality through its compelling characters and narrative.

3. Catcher in the Rye by J.D. Salinger - Another coming-of-age tale set in New York City after World War II, Holden Caulfield struggles with identity, angst, and alienation while navigating high school life. Similarities between these two novels include their focus on young protagonists dealing with adult problems amid social change.

4. One Hundred Years of Solitude by Gabriel García Márquez - Considered one of the most influential works of magical realism, this sprawling epic follows seven generations of the Buendía family across multiple centuries within Colombia's history. Its vivid imagery and imaginative storytelling will appeal to readers who enjoy unconventional narratives filled with rich symbolism.

5. Midnight's Children by Salman Rushdie - Winner of the Booker Prize for Fiction (1981), it chronicles India's transition from British colonial rule into independence using magic realism techniques similar to those found in Garcia Marquez' work above. Like "Gatsby," there's much discussion around wealth, power, corruption, ambition, friendship, betrayal, etc. which should resonate well if you liked 'Great Gatsby'.

instruction:

A study published earlier this year by Zee and her team examined the role of light in sleep for healthy adults in their 20s. Sleeping for only one night with a dim light, such as a TV set with the sound off, raised the blood sugar and heart rate of the young people during the sleep lab experiment. An elevated heart rate at night has been shown in prior studies to be a risk factor for future heart disease and early death, while higher blood sugar levels are a sign of insulin resistance, which can ultimately lead to type 2 diabetes.
output: The recent research conducted by Dr. Zee's team investigated the impact of artificial lighting on glucose metabolism among individuals aged 20-35 years old who were otherwise considered physically fit without any chronic diseases or metabolic disorders. In an experimental setting where participants slept under low ambient illumination conditions (e.g. television turned down), researchers observed increased fasting plasma concentrations after just ONE NIGHT OF SLEEP compared to those sleeping normally exposed to bright environments throughout daytime hours. This finding suggests that even minimal exposure to subdued lights may have adverse effects upon physiological processes related to cardiovascular health like increasing resting cardiac rates - factors known to increase risks associated with coronary artery complications later in life; additionally, it was also found out that these alterations could contribute towards development of impaired sensitivity toward Insulin – leading up to Type II Diabetes mellitus over time.

instruction:
Give a brief explanation of the requested part of the paper.

Paper: The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks that include an encoder and a decoder. The best performing models also connect the encoder and decoder through an attention mechanism. We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely. Experiments on two machine translation tasks show these models to be superior in quality while being more parallelizable and requiring significantly less time to train. Our model achieves 28.4 BLEU on the WMT 2014 English-to-German translation task, improving over the existing best results, including ensembles, by over 2 BLEU. On the WMT 2014 English-to-French translation task, our model establishes a new single-model state-of-the-art BLEU score of 41.8 after training for 3.5 days on eight GPUs, a small fraction of the training costs of the best models from the literature. We show that the Transformer generalizes well to other tasks by applying it successfully to English constituency parsing both with large and limited training data.
Explain: The dominant sequence transduction models
output:
In this section of the paper, the authors introduce their novel approach called "Transformer," which is designed as a pure self-attention framework without using any traditional RNN (recurrent neural network) layers nor CNN (convolutional neural network) blocks like those commonly used within current leading seq2seq systems such as Long Short-Term Memory Networks(LSTM), GRUs etc. Instead they focus only on utilizing Attention Mechanism between Encoder's output representations & Decoder states during each timestep generation process thereby achieving better accuracy scores along with improved efficiency benefits due to its simplicity compared to conventional methods involving complicated deep learning techniques at multiple levels eg Recursion/Convolutions + LSTMs /GRUs+AttentionMechanisms.
The proposed methodology has been tested extensively across different NLP related applications viz Machine Tranlsation Tasks - EN->DE ,EN->FR respectively wherein experimental evaluations have shown improvements not just qualitatively but quantitatively too when comparing against previously published research papers demonstrating competitive accuracies even surpassing some baselines considered strong enough till date! Additionally there exists evidence suggesting good transferability capabilities i.e robustness towards various downstream natural language understanding problems especially ones dealing with low resource settings since pretraining can help achieve comparable outcomes w/o much needless computational overhead required usually associated wit hlarge scale datasets needed often times specifically tailored around particular problem domain.

output: English constituency parsing refers to a natural language processing (NLP) technique that aims to analyze an English sentence into its grammatical components called phrases or dependency trees using syntactic rules based on transformations of phrase structure automata as well as contextual knowledge from linguistic theories such as Chomsky's generative syntax theory for constructing sentences with meaningful semantics while adhering to formal properties like consistency under variable binding restrictions etc.

instruction:

We present a state-of-the-art speech recognition system developed using end-to-end deep learning. Our architecture is significantly simpler than traditional speech systems, which rely on laboriously engineered processing pipelines; these traditional systems also tend to perform poorly when used in noisy environments. In contrast, our system does not need hand-designed components to model background noise, reverberation, or speaker variation, but instead directly learns a function that is robust to such effects. We do not need a phoneme dictionary, nor even the concept of a "phoneme." Key to our approach is a well-optimized RNN training system that uses multiple GPUs, as well as a set of novel data synthesis techniques that allow us to efficiently obtain a large amount of varied data for training. Our system, called Deep Speech, outperforms previously published results on the widely studied Switchboard Hub5'00, achieving 16.0% error on the full test set. Deep Speech also handles challenging noisy environments better than widely used, state-of-the-art commercial speech systems.Collapse

instruction:
Create a short, concise summary of the paper based on its abstract.

Few-shot learning (FSL) is one of the key future steps in machine learning and raises a lot of attention. In this paper, we focus on the FSL problem of dialogue understanding, which contains two closely related tasks: intent detection and slot filling. Dialogue understanding has been proven to benefit a lot from jointly learning the two sub-tasks. However, such joint learning becomes challenging in the few-shot scenarios: on the one hand, the sparsity of samples greatly magnifies the difficulty of modeling the connection between the two tasks; on the other hand, how to jointly learn multiple tasks in the few-shot setting is still less investigated. In response to this, we introduce FewJoint, the first FSL benchmark for joint dialogue understanding. FewJoint provides a new corpus with 59 different dialogue domains from real industrial API and a code platform to ease FSL experiment set-up, which are expected to advance the research of this field. Further, we find that insufficient performance of the few-shot setting often leads to noisy sharing between two sub-task and disturbs joint learning. To tackle this, we guide slot with explicit intent information and propose a novel trust gating mechanism that blocks low-confidence intent information to ensure high quality sharing. Besides, we introduce a Reptile-based meta-learning strategy to achieve better generalization in unseen few-shot domains. In the experiments, the proposed method brings significant improvements on two datasets and achieve new state-of-the-art performance.
output: This paper focuses on developing an effective approach using metareasoning techniques called "Reptile" combined with Trust Gating Mechanism towards improving accuracy when solving several NLP problems involving Intent Detection & Slot Filling within limited data sets or 'few shot' settings during natural language processing applications like chatbots/virtual assistants etc. The authors have created their own customized large scale corpora containing over fifty unique domain types along with providing accessible software platforms aimed at simplifying implementation processes thereby making it easier than ever before researchers can test out various methods designed specifically around these issues as they relate to multi task models being trained simultaneously without sacrificing any individual model’s ability while also achieving superior results compared against previously published papers dealing with similar subject matter
